mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at p < 0.10 in OLS regression).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion periods (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payrolls indicate robust employment growth and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS levels show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently near zero across multiple macro cycles).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals underlying demand resilience, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when consumption outpaces GDP revisions.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates during recessionary transitions signal prolonged economic weakness, suppressing corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE levels fail to predict negative 60-day returns during recessionary periods (mean forward return > 0% when UNRATE > 7%).
